phases.name: Make typed composition real
phases.intent: Enforce the agent input schema contracts that currently exist as decorative annotations, and demonstrate template composition across multiple agent families — converting the typed composition advantage from aspirational to functional. (pre-FGAP-026 planning artifact)

success_criteria.criterion: executeAgentStep validates input against AgentSpec.inputSchema before dispatch
All existing workflow specs pass input schema validation without error
At least 3 template families use Nunjucks extends/block composition (up from 1)
At least one reusable agent accepts variable input shapes via optional fields with conditional template rendering

specs.name: Enforce agent input schema validation
specs.intent: Add schema validation in executeAgentStep so typed contracts are real

specs.name: Fix workflow specs for schema compliance
specs.intent: Ensure every agent invocation in existing workflows satisfies the agent's input schema

specs.name: Expand template inheritance to more agent families
specs.intent: Prove composition model works beyond the single analyzer family

specs.name: Redesign reusable agents with parametric input
specs.intent: Transform structural repetition into genuine parametric recombination

phases.name: Expand capabilities
phases.intent: Add ad-hoc agent invocation (bridging the pi-subagents usability gap) and worktree isolation for parallel steps (preventing filesystem conflicts in concurrent execution). (pre-FGAP-026 planning artifact)

success_criteria.criterion: /workflow run-agent dispatches a single agent step without a workflow spec
parallel: steps with worktree: true create isolated git worktrees per sub-step

specs.name: Ad-hoc single-agent invocation
specs.intent: Allow agent dispatch from command line or tool call without a .workflow.yaml

specs.name: Worktree isolation for parallel steps
specs.intent: Prevent filesystem conflicts when parallel agents write files concurrently

phases.name: Future-proofing
phases.intent: Add safety infrastructure (recursion depth guard) and deferred capabilities (context forking, async execution) required before Phase 6 nested workflow invocation can be unblocked. (pre-FGAP-026 planning artifact)

success_criteria.criterion: PI_WORKFLOW_MAX_DEPTH env var limits nested workflow invocation depth

specs.name: Workflow recursion depth guard
specs.intent: Prevent unbounded recursive workflow spawning via env-var depth tracking

phases.name: Task execution loop
phases.intent: Close the planning-to-execution gap: workflows produce task blocks but nothing consumes them as work input. Build the do-task workflow and supporting agents that read a task entry, investigate its scope, implement it, verify against acceptance criteria, and update task status — completing the self-driving cycle from plan-from-requirements through to done. (pre-FGAP-026 planning artifact)

success_criteria.criterion: A workflow reads a task entry from .project/tasks.json by ID and executes it through to status update
Agent templates accept task block fields (description, acceptance_criteria, files, depends_on, phase)
Task status lifecycle is updated by the workflow (planned → in-progress → completed/blocked)
Verification step checks acceptance_criteria from the task entry, not just agent-generated criteria

specs.name: do-task workflow
specs.intent: Read task entry by ID, investigate scope, decompose if needed, implement, verify against acceptance_criteria, update task status

specs.name: Task-aware agent templates
specs.intent: Agents that accept task block entry content — description, acceptance_criteria, files, depends_on — and adapt their behavior accordingly

specs.name: Batch task execution
specs.intent: forEach over planned tasks with dependency ordering — execute tasks in topological order respecting depends_on

phases.name: FGAP-026 closure phase 1 — resolver foundation + discovery tools
phases.intent: Resolver foundation (resolveContextDir) + 2 discovery tools (read-config, read-schema) + atomic safety .pi-context.json write. Step 8.7.1.

phases.name: FGAP-026 closure phase 2 — high-impact query tools
phases.intent: filter-block-items, resolve-items-by-id (bulk), walk-ancestors, walk-by-relation, find-references. Step 8.7.2.

phases.name: FGAP-026 closure phase 3 — work-unit context composition
phases.intent: Work-unit context composition substrate per DEC-0017 — context-contract substrate (FGAP-030) + gather-execution-context primitive (FGAP-031). Step 8.7.3.

phases.name: FGAP-026 closure phase 4 — workflow-execute + dispatch tools
phases.intent: workflow-execute + workflow-resume dispatch tools — harness-confined main LLM path to bash/read/write under DEC-0014 via JIT-agent dispatch through workflow steps. Step 8.7.4.

phases.name: FGAP-026 closure phase 5 — arc-tracking dogfood substrate
phases.intent: Arc-tracking blocks authored as dogfood substrate per FGAP-028 — config + relations + roadmap + phase + context-contracts for THIS repo. Step 8.7.5; partially realized through the FGAP-046/060 arc.

phases.name: FGAP-046/060/DEC-0028 coordinated arc
phases.intent: Eliminate inline FK (DEC-0013 enforcement) so the FGAP-060 canonical_id rename engine operates on one clean edge surface. Couples FGAP-040 + FGAP-046 + FGAP-049 + FGAP-060 + validateProject edge-migration + phase array-block (DEC-0028).
